Peak Lists Climbers Ranges Geography Help Search Log In
 Logged in: Alex Doe  |  My Home Page  |  Edit Account  |  Add Ascent  |  My Ascents  |  My List of Lists  |  Add Peak to DB  |  My Peaks  |  Query  |  Personal Lists

Peakbagging Page for Alex Doe

Personal Climb Logs

The links below take you to dynamically generated lists of Alex Doe's peakbagging activity.

  • Ascent List: List of Alex Doe's hikes, climbs, summits, and attempts.
    •  Ascent Subset Page: Filter ascents using various criteria.
  • Peak List: List of distinct summits attained, with peak statistics. Includes a peak's "Personal Isolation".
  • List of Lists: Peakbagging Lists that Alex Doe is pursuing, showing progress for each list by number and percent of peaks climbed.
  • Trip Report Index: Same as the main climb list, but only showing climbs with trip reports and/or GPS Tracks.
  • Unsuccessful Ascent List: A badge of honor for real climbers--lessons learned when turning back.
  • Multiple Ascents List: A listing of all peaks climbed more than once.
  • Progressive Peak List: Time-ordered progressive lists for eight different metrics.
  • Peak Pairs and Unique Peaks: Peaks and peak pairs that no other registered site users have climbed.
  • First Ascents: Shows both true first ascents, and first ascents among those logged by Peakbagger.com registered users.
  • Master Peak Map: Interactive worldwide map showing all peaks climbed, color-coded by elevation or prominence.

Social Networking

Reports showing a climber's buddies and other climbers. Click for More Info

  • Buddy List: List of Alex Doe's hiking/climbing partners
  • Reverse Buddy List: Climbers with Alex Doe on their buddy lists

  • NEW! Companion Report: Shows key summary stats for all hiking/climbing partner data that was logged for ascents

  • Compare Climbers: Compare the ascents of up to 5 climbers

Personal Lists

Lists personally created by Alex Doe. (Search for Lists from other climbers).

  • Personal Lists: Shows all lists created by Alex Doe. Includes Query Lists created using the Query Tool, and "Pick-and-Choose" Lists created by selecting individual peaks.
    • Search for Lists: Search across all lists hosted on the site, both "main" lists and those created by all climbers.
  • Query Tool: Create your own peak lists by running custom queries against the Peakbagger.com database.
  • New Pick-and-Choose List: Create a custom peak list by individually selecting any set of peaks from the site's database. Click for More Info.
  • Wish List: A list of unclimbed peaks that are high-priority and/or have immediate climbing plans.

Time-Period Summary Reports

These reports show number of peaks climbed, highest point reached, and other statistics grouped by year or month.

  • Snapshot Year/Month Grid: A concise year/month grid showing significant climbs.
  • List Progress by Year for Highest Priority Lists: Number of peaks climbed by list and year, for top 20 lists by user-defined priority.
  • Prominence Summary - Totals by Year: Number of peaks climbed by prominence cutoff and year.
  • Snapshot Year/Place Grids: Grids with region breakdowns:
       West USA  East USA  N.A./World  Europe  Europe/World
  • Year Summary Report: Year-by-year climbing summary.
  • Year-Month Summary Report: Month-by-Month climbing summary.
  • Month Summary Report: Climbing summary by month across all years.
  • Days with Ascents Grid: Shows all 366 days and which have any ascents.

User Created Content

  • Add a Peak to the database that is not already present.
  • List of uploaded photos uploaded to the site.
  • Upload a Photo with a caption and map it to Peaks and Ascents.

P-Index: 95Link to Peak List sorted by prominence
I-Index: 21Link to Peak List sorted by isolation
E-Index: 95Link to Peak List sorted by elevation
Ascents: 163Count of all logged ascents
Peaks/Points: 137Count of distinct peaks/points climbed
Peaks on Lists: 94Count of distinct peaks that are on lists

Trip Reports: 75Count of posted trip reports
Days with Ascents: 80Days with a new 100m prominence peak

Contact this Climber

You can send a private message to this climber by typing in the box below and clicking the "Send Email" button. Your email address will be visible and they can reply directly to you. Of course, this site never shares any email addresses without consent, and anyone may choose to ignore private messages.
